items.q: How do you say 'I used the computer today'? (Spain Spanish)
items.why: In Spain, HOY (today) and recent time markers (esta semana, este mes) trigger PRETÉRITO PERFECTO (he + participle). Latin America uses preterite for these. Common error in essays: 'esta mañana fui' instead of 'esta mañana he ido'.
Students apply English pronunciation. Spanish H is ALWAYS silent. 'Hola' is 'OH-lah', not 'HO-lah'. (The breathy sound students associate with H comes from j or g+e/i in Spanish.)

items.q: Why does 'inglés' have a written accent?
items.correct: It stresses the final syllable while ending in -s, which is unusual.
items.misconception: All Spanish words have accent marks.
items.why: Students treat accents as random. They follow STRESS RULES: words stressed normally don't need accents; the accent shows when stress falls outside the default position (or distinguishes homophones).

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my sister'?
items.correct: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.misconception: Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.
items.random_wrong: Yo llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.. “Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is the correct use of ser/estar: 'I am tired'?
items.why: It's Estoy cansado.. “Soy cansado.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many forms does the Spanish adjective 'simpático' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur)?
items.why: The real figure is 4 forms. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
